mnemonics.title: WEB-INF Contents
mnemonics.content: CLW: Classes, Lib, web.xml - Three essential components under WEB-INF directory

mnemonics.title: Deployment Sequence
mnemonics.content: SDS: Stop Server → Deploy WAR → Start Server - Proper deployment order

mnemonics.title: Servlet Lifecycle
mnemonics.content: ISD: init() → service() → destroy() - Tomcat's servlet phase sequence

acronyms.usage: Remember deployment package format containing web resources
Core components: Servlet Container hosts Applications via Tomcat
Essential deployment sequence for WAR files

visualTips.visualization: Imagine Tomcat as a house: WEB-INF = locked basement (secure), classes = kitchen (processing), lib = pantry (ingredients)
Picture a zipped suitcase with web pages (clothes), servlets (tools), and libs (accessories) packed inside
8080 as main gate (HTTP), 8005 as emergency exit (shutdown), 8009 as backdoor (AJP connector)
Visualize a car dashboard with deploy/undeploy buttons as gear controls and WAR list as odometer

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant kitchen - receives orders (HTTP requests), processes them (servlets), serves meals (responses)
Similar to installing a vending machine - stop power (shutdown), install new machine (deploy WAR), restart
Like a restaurant menu - lists available dishes (servlets) and their recipes (URL mappings)
Acts like a post office - receives letters (requests), routes to correct department (servlet), sends replies (responses)
